title: AgentBreeder Memory Configuration
description: Schema for memory.yaml — the memory backend definition file for AgentBreeder

description.description: Short description of the memory configuration

team.description: Team that owns this memory config

backend.description: Storage backend for memory. 'in_memory' was removed in v1.9 — use 'postgresql'.

memory_type.description: Memory strategy type. 'buffer_window' and 'buffer' are sliding/unlimited window types. 'summary' condenses old messages automatically. 'entity' extracts named entities and injects them as context. 'semantic' stores embeddings for similarity search (Phase 2: embeddings are stubbed; real vectors in Phase 3).

max_messages.description: Maximum messages to retain (for buffer_window type)

ttl_seconds.description: Time-to-live for stored messages in seconds (0 = no expiry). Injected as MEMORY_TTL_SECONDS into the agent container at deploy time.

summary_trigger_threshold.description: For memory_type=summary: condense into a summary row when the session reaches this many non-summary messages.

scope.description: Memory isolation scope. 'agent' is per-agent (default). 'team' shares memory across agents in the same team and enforces team ownership on load/save. 'global' is org-wide (Phase 3 — not yet available; the API will return HTTP 400 if used).
